texto: I haven’t entirely adjusted to the whole yum, blood, yum aspect of being a vampire. My body wants it, but my head is still like, Ew, that is BLOOD, time to faint.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tamara Summers: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “I haven’t entirely adjusted to the whole yum, blood, yum aspect of being a vampire. My body wants…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, blood, vampire pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de vampire.

texto: If you were dying...If you were sixteen and dying...If your blood was spilling out of you, calling to them, the creatures of the night, and you knew you were dying...If you saw their pale faces and the gleam of sharp teeth in the moonlight, and you felt your blood spilling warmly over your hands, and you knew beyond any doubt that you were dying...Wouldn’t you say yes?Yes, turn me.Yes, I want to live.Yes... make me one of you.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Tamara Summers que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “If you were dying...If you were sixteen and dying...If your blood was spilling out of you, callin…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre vida, dying, turning-into-a-vampire, com foco em boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

texto: Sure, I had been accused of murder by my family and was wrapped up in a bizarre investigation, but there were such cute boys involved. Hey, I try to look on the bright side.

texto: You have to understand, the blood we drink every day to stay alive comes out of a jar in the refrigerator. It is the very definition of gross.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Tamara Summers sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida.
